airplane: A passenger airplane with a white fuselage, swept-back wings, and visible jet engines mounted under the wings.
An airplane viewed from the side, showing its streamlined body, tail fin with horizontal stabilizers, and a row of windows along the fuselage.
The commercial airliner features a distinctive nose cone, cockpit windshield, and landing gear tucked beneath the belly.
A top-down view of an airplane on the tarmac reveals its cross-shaped silhouette with fuselage, wings, and tail assembly.
The 3D shape consists of a long cylindrical main body, two thin symmetrical wing surfaces extending outward, and a vertical tail fin.
Point cloud of an airplane exhibits dense, evenly distributed points along the fuselage tube and sparse planar patches for the wings.
The geometry is dominated by bilateral symmetry, with the mirror-symmetric wings meeting the central fuselage at a consistent dihedral angle.
Aircraft point distribution shows a concentrated high-density nose area, tapering tail cone, and thin trailing edges on wings and stabilizers.
The smooth metallic skin of the airplane fuselage visible in photographs corresponds to a continuous swept cylinder in the 3D geometric scan.
The painted airline livery and cabin windows seen in images are absent in the pure point cloud, which retains only the underlying aerodynamic shape.
bathtub: A bathtub is a large, deep basin usually made of white porcelain or acrylic, featuring a curved interior and often accompanied by a faucet and drain.
From a 2D image, a bathtub appears as a smooth, glossy, elongated container with a distinctive oval or rectangular shape, typically set against bathroom tiles.
Common visual features of a bathtub include a rolled rim, a sloping backrest, and metallic fixtures such as a handheld shower head or spout.
In photographs, bathtubs are characterized by their reflective white surface, water stains or soap suds, and the surrounding context of a bathroom with towel racks and vanity.
In a 3D point cloud, a bathtub exhibits a pronounced concave basin with a smooth, sweeping interior surface that slopes toward a central drain location.
The geometric structure of a bathtub is characterized by bilateral symmetry along its long axis, with the point cloud forming a hollow shell around an open top cavity.
Points densely cover the outer shell and inner bowl, delineating the rounded rim at the upper edge and the flat or gently curved floor of the tub.
From a top-down view, the point distribution outlines an oval or rectangular perimeter, with the deepest points clustered along the midline and tapering toward the edges.
The smooth, curved interior of a bathtub that captures highlights in images corresponds to the concave, bowl-shaped surface densely sampled in the point cloud.
Visual elements like the faucet deck and overflow drain observed in photographs align with distinct clusters of points on the rim and inner wall in the 3D reconstruction.
bed: A bed typically features a large rectangular mattress covered with sheets and blankets, often accompanied by pillows and a headboard against a wall.
In images, beds are recognizable by their soft cushioned surface, wooden or metal frames, and cozy bedroom settings with nightstands nearby.
The appearance of a bed includes a prominent horizontal sleeping area, sometimes with a decorative headboard and footboard, conveying comfort and rest.
Visual cues for a bed include a flat elevated platform with bedding, often aligned against a wall in a dimly lit room suggesting a sleeping environment.
The 3D structure of a bed consists of a large flat rectangular prism for the mattress, supported by a lower frame, with a taller vertical slab at one short end as the headboard.
A bed's point cloud reveals a dominant horizontal planar region at a low elevation, with parallel side edges and a bilateral symmetry along its longest axis.
From a top-down view, the bed exhibits a rectangular distribution of points, with dense planar areas on the mattress top and sparse vertical points for legs and headboard.
Geometrically, a bed is an assemblage of a thick box-like volume resting on a rectangular base, often with a perpendicular planar extension at the head.
Combining visual and geometric traits, a bed appears as a soft-looking horizontal surface atop a rigid frame, its point cloud capturing the flat mattress plane and upright headboard slab.
Whether in an image or a 3D scan, a bed is defined by its long rectangular platform with a vertical backrest, merging textile textures in 2D with planar geometry in 3D.
bench: A wooden bench with a slatted seat and backrest, often seen in parks, featuring a natural brown wood texture.
A metal park bench with a curved back, black paint, and visible armrests, set against a grassy background.
A long wooden picnic bench with attached table, showing planks of light-colored wood and a rustic outdoor setting.
A minimalist concrete bench in an urban plaza, with a smooth gray surface and low profile, devoid of backrest.
The point cloud reveals an elongated rectangular seat plane supported by four vertical legs at corners, with a parallel backrest plane extending upward at a slight angle.
The structure consists of two horizontal planes: a wide seat and narrower backrest, connected by short vertical struts, forming an L-shape in side profile.
A symmetric arrangement of points shows a central seat plank flanked by two armrest planes at each end, with all components aligned along the long axis.
The geometry is a planar rectangular shape with uniform thickness, elevated from the ground by evenly spaced cylindrical legs, exhibiting bilateral symmetry.

The wooden slatted bench seen in photographs translates to a repeating pattern of narrow horizontal bars in the point cloud, with gaps between the slats forming a recognizable ribbed texture on the seat and backrest.

From a side view, the bench's L-shaped silhouette in images matches the point cloud's two perpendicular planes—the seat and the backrest—while frontal views reveal the symmetrical leg arrangement and armrest overhangs.
bookshelf: A bookshelf is a piece of furniture with multiple horizontal shelves, often made of wood or metal, used for storing books and decorative items.
In a typical photograph, a bookshelf appears as a rectangular unit with visible shelf edges, filled with colorful book spines of varying sizes.
The front view of a bookshelf reveals rows of books aligned along each shelf, with occasional gaps and decorative objects interspersed.
A bookshelf often features a back panel, side panels, and sometimes an open design, with the texture of wood grain or painted surface visible in images.
From a 3D point cloud, a bookshelf is characterized by a set of parallel horizontal planar surfaces supported by vertical planar sides, forming an open-front rectangular prism.
The point cloud of a bookshelf shows a dense, flat distribution of points on each shelf and back panel, with sparse points representing the gaps between shelves.
Geometrically, a bookshelf exhibits symmetry along its vertical midline and repetitive horizontal layering of shelf planes.
In a point cloud, the overall structure is a box-like frame with interior horizontal partitions, and it often lacks points on the front face due to openness, revealing depth.
The visual appearance of a filled bookshelf translates into a 3D point cloud as a rectangular volume with horizontal shelf layers and clustered point densities where books are present.
A bookshelf's image of orderly rows of books corresponds to a geometric pattern of parallel planes and varied point densities along the depth axis in a point cloud.
bottle: A transparent or colored glass container with a narrow neck and a wider body, often used for holding liquids.
A cylindrical object with a bottleneck and a label, commonly seen on store shelves.
A tall drinking vessel with a curved silhouette, capped with a metallic or plastic lid.
An everyday household item made of plastic or glass, featuring a screw-on cap and a curved shoulder.
A roughly symmetrical shape with a cylindrical body tapering to a smaller neck, exhibiting rotational symmetry around the vertical axis.
A hollow tubular structure with a flared base and a constricted top, resulting in a point cloud that is denser around the cylindrical midsection.
A 3D object composed of a main elongated cylinder topped by a narrower cylindrical segment, possibly with a flared lip at the opening.
An axis-aligned geometry with a pointed cloud distribution that emphasizes the curved transition from the body to the neck.
Its visual appearance of a container with a neck is reflected in its 3D shape as a main cylinder narrowing at the top.
From images, we recognize a bottle by its label and cap, while the point cloud reveals its consistent radial symmetry and hollow interior.
bowl: A bowl typically appears as a round, concave vessel with a wide opening, often seen in images with smooth ceramic surfaces, symmetrical rim, and sometimes decorative patterns or glossy reflections.
In photographs, a bowl is recognizable by its curved interior wall that catches light and shadow, a flat base that rests on surfaces, and its common association with food items like soup or cereal.
A bowl often displays a simple, utilitarian design with a continuous, unbroken rim and a deep basin, frequently depicted in warm colors such as white, beige, or blue in household contexts.
From a top-down view, a bowl presents a perfect circle with concentric highlights, while side views reveal a gentle arc from rim to base, making it distinct from plates or cups.

The 3D geometry of a bowl is characterized by a hemispherical or paraboloid shell with a hollow interior, symmetric around a central vertical axis, and a thick, solid base that forms a flat or slightly concave bottom.
In point cloud data, a bowl exhibits a radially symmetric point distribution, with points densely covering the inner concave surface and outer convex surface, merging at a sharp circular rim.

The bowl's structure in 3D reconstruction shows two distinct zones: a concave internal cavity that deepens towards the center, and a convex external curvature that tapers smoothly down to a thickened foot ring.

A bowl's shape can be described by a set of points forming a circular opening at the top, gradually narrowing into a rounded bottom, with no sharp edges except at the rim; the overall point cloud captures this continuous double-curved surface.

The visual smoothness and glossy highlights seen in images correspond to the 3D bowl’s fine curvature and the lack of surface discontinuities, resulting in a continuous, doubly curved point cloud surface.

From both 2D appearance and 3D geometry, a bowl is defined by its deep, rounded cavity and wide rim, which in images creates shaded interior views and in point clouds forms a hollow, axisymmetric volume.
car: A car is a wheeled motor vehicle with a distinct body, windows, headlights, and tail lights, often seen on roads with a glossy painted exterior.
The vehicle typically features a windshield, side mirrors, doors, and a grille at the front, with tires that have tread patterns.
From a side view, a car shows a sloping roofline, a trunk or hatchback, and a streamlined shape that reduces air resistance.
The image of a car often includes reflective surfaces, license plates, and interior details visible through windows.
A car's point cloud reveals a rectangular footprint with rounded corners, a low height above the ground, and symmetrical left-right hull.
The 3D scan shows distinct wheel arches, a flat undercarriage, and a smooth, continuous roof surface that slopes downward at the rear.
From a top-down view, the point distribution forms an elongated shape with two parallel sides, and the wheels appear as circular clusters near the corners.
The car's geometry includes a protruding front bumper, a slightly elevated rear deck, and a void where the cabin windows would be, capturing partial transparency in lidar.
In both images and point clouds, a car is defined by its boxy cabin, four wheels, and aerodynamic curves that transition smoothly from visual textures to geometric surfaces.
The visual identity of a car, with its shiny finish and brand emblem, corresponds to a dense point cluster forming a rigid, hollow shell with precise symmetries.
chair: A chair is a piece of furniture with a horizontal seat, a vertical backrest, and typically four legs, often used for sitting.
In a 2D image, a chair is recognizable by its distinct silhouette featuring a flat seating surface and a supportive back structure.
Visually, chairs come in many styles like armchairs, dining chairs, or stools, often upholstered or made of wood, metal, or plastic.
A typical chair appears as an object with a seat at roughly knee height, a backrest rising from it, and sometimes armrests on the sides.
In a 3D point cloud, a chair consists of a planar seat region, cylindrical leg points, and a backrest plane perpendicular to the seat.
The point cloud of a chair exhibits bilateral symmetry, with legs distributed evenly under a rectangular seat and a centered backrest.
Geometrically, a chair's point cloud shows a flat, elevated surface supported by vertical extrusions at its corners and a tall, thin back panel.
A chair's 3D structure often includes a seat with a regular boundary, four leg clusters forming a rectangular footprint, and a planar backrest above the seat's rear edge.
The visual appearance of a chair’s seat and back, often textured and colored, corresponds to two orthogonal planar regions in a 3D point cloud.
In both images and point clouds, a chair's legs are visually elongated forms that translate to vertical point clusters, connecting the seat to the ground.
cone: A cone is typically seen as a traffic cone with a bright orange color and white reflective stripes, often used for road safety warnings.
The object appears as a conical shape with a wide circular base and a pointed tip, commonly found in construction zones.
In photographic images, a cone can be recognized by its distinct orange hue and the segmented pattern of alternating colored bands.
A cone may also refer to an ice cream cone, featuring a crispy waffle texture and a spherical scoop of ice cream on top.
From a 3D point cloud perspective, a cone is defined by a circular base that tapers smoothly to a single apex point.
The point cloud of a cone exhibits rotational symmetry around its central axis, with points densely clustered along the curved surface.
A cone's geometry comprises a flat circular base and a curved lateral surface that narrows gradually towards a sharp tip.
In a 3D scan, a cone can be identified by the distribution of points forming a circular cross-section that decreases in radius linearly along the height.
The visual appearance of a bright orange cone with a white stripe corresponds to a 3D conical geometry with a hollow base and a pointed top.
An object described as a cone in images, whether a traffic marker or a party hat, shares a pyramidal-like 3D structure with a curved surface ending in a point.
cup: A cup is a small open container with a circular rim, a curved handle attached to its side, and a smooth or slightly textured surface that can be ceramic, glass, or metal.
In images, a cup often appears as a short, hollow cylinder with a handle protruding outward, frequently placed on a saucer or table, and often filled with a beverage like coffee or tea.
Common visual features of a cup include a wide circular opening at the top, a smaller flat base, decorative patterns, logos, or solid colors, and a curved handle on one side.
A typical cup has a bright or neutral-colored body with a glossy or matte finish, vertical sidewalls, and a distinct handle loop that is clearly visible from most 2D perspectives.
In 3D point clouds, a cup is characterized by a hollow cylindrical body with a uniform or slightly tapered radius, a flat bottom disk, and an attached toroidal or swept handle structure.
The geometric structure of a cup consists of a vertical cylindrical shell open at the top, a circular base plane, and a protruding loop handle formed by a closed curve sweep.
From a 3D geometry perspective, a cup is an axis-symmetric hollow cylinder missing its top face, with a handle that is an elongated torus segment bridging two points on the outer wall.

Point cloud scans of a cup reveal a dense distribution of points on the outer cylindrical surface, sparse interior points near the opening, and a ring of points forming the handle that connects to the body.
Visually, a cup appears as a handled cylinder; in 3D, its point cloud captures the hollow cavity, the smooth curvature of the sidewall, and the looped appendage of the handle.
The 2D image of a cup shows a round rim and a handle, which correspond to the 3D geometry of an open cylindrical shell with a C-shaped handle attached by two junctions.
curtain: A curtain is a piece of fabric that hangs vertically to cover a window, often with gathered or pleated folds creating rhythmic vertical lines.
The curtain's visible surface shows a repeating pattern or solid color, with top edge attached to a rod via rings or a pocket, and bottom edge hanging freely.
From a front view, a curtain typically drapes in soft, wavy undulations that catch light and shadow, revealing its textile nature.
Curtains often frame a window or appear in pairs, with a valance or header at the top adding a decorative horizontal element.
The point cloud of a curtain consists of a thin, undulating surface with slight thickness, suspended from a nearly straight top boundary.
In 3D, the curtain exhibits a series of parallel, vertical fold ridges that alternate in depth, creating a wave-like profile when viewed from above.
The curtain's geometry is characterized by a roughly rectangular planar layout that is deformed into deep, periodic corrugations along its width.
From a side view, the point cloud reveals a sequence of convex and concave arcs corresponding to the drape folds, with no rigid internal structure.
The cloth-like appearance and draped folds in 2D images are directly reflected in the 3D point cloud as a thin, non-planar surface with alternating ridges and valleys.
Visual cues such as highlight and shadow on the curtain's fabric correspond to depth variations in the point cloud that define the rhythmic fold geometry.
desk: A wooden desk with a broad rectangular top, four straight legs, and sometimes a drawer or hutch for storage.
An office desk featuring a smooth work surface, often accompanied by a computer monitor, keyboard, and desk lamp.
A traditional school desk with an attached chair, a slanted writing surface, and a compartment for books.
A modern minimalist desk with a clean white surface, metal hairpin legs, and no visible clutter.
The point cloud depicts a large, flat horizontal rectangle supported by four vertical cylindrical or rectangular prismatic legs at the corners.
The geometry consists of a thin planar surface parallel to the ground, with four elongated vertical elements with constant cross-section.
The structure is bilaterally symmetric about a central axis, with two legs on each side forming a stable base under the planar top.
The point distribution shows a dense planar region for the desktop with sparse vertical lines corresponding to the legs, and occasionally a boxy volume for a drawer unit.
What appears as a solid wooden desk in images becomes a set of flat rectangles and linear legs in the 3D scan, with sharp corners and a thin horizontal slab.
Visual cues like a wood grain texture or metallic legs correspond to geometric regions: a textured planar top and smooth cylindrical point clusters for metal legs.
door: A rectangular vertical panel with a handle, hinges, and sometimes a lock, commonly found in walls and used as an entryway.
The door exhibits a smooth or textured front surface with distinctive features like a door knob, peephole, and possibly decorative panels.
From a two-dimensional view, the door appears as a flat plane with a contrasting frame, often featuring a kick plate at the bottom.
A door can be identified in images by its surrounding frame, a threshold at the base, and a swing direction indicated by hinge placement.
A thin rectangular prismatic volume with sharp edges and a uniform thickness, typically oriented vertically and attached to a wall plane.
The point cloud of a door reveals a dense cluster of points forming a planar surface, with cutouts for handles and hinge recesses creating deviations from flatness.
Structurally, the door is a flat object with bilateral symmetry across its central vertical axis, often featuring a lower density of points near the handle due to the opening.

In a 3D scan, the door is represented as a detached or partially attached panel, with a narrow gap along its edges where it meets the frame, and points distributed concentrically around the hinge axis.
The door appears in images as a painted or wooden slab, which in 3D corresponds to a thin volumetric block with surface normals emphasizing its flat front face and narrow sides.

Visually, a door shows a handle on one side and hinges on the other, while the point cloud captures these as small protruding or indented regions that disrupt the overall planarity of the rectangular panel.
dresser: A wooden dresser with multiple drawers, often featuring decorative handles and a smooth polished surface, commonly found in bedrooms for storing clothes.
The dresser typically has a rectangular silhouette with a flat top surface, sometimes accompanied by an attached mirror, and drawers arranged in columns.
From a 2D image perspective, a dresser presents a front-facing arrangement of evenly spaced horizontal drawer fronts with knobs or pulls at the center.
A dresser is a piece of furniture with a broad, flat top often used to hold items like photos or lamps, and its drawers provide organized storage.
As a 3D point cloud, the dresser exhibits a boxy main body with protrusions that represent drawer handles, and distinct planar faces for each drawer front.
The point cloud reveals a structured stack of rectangular drawer volumes within a larger rectangular frame, with gaps or indents at drawer boundaries visible as sharp edges.
Geometrically, the dresser is composed of a dominant horizontal rectangular prism as the top, supported by a slightly smaller vertical cuboid body segmented by fine linear gaps where drawers meet.

In 3D, the dresser's point distribution is dense on the flat front faces, with occasional sparse points on handles creating small circular clusters, and the sides forming planar surfaces connected at right angles.
The dresser's visual appearance of a smooth wooden front aligns with the 3D point cloud's flat, planar drawer faces and small, knob-like protrusions for handles.
From image to geometry, the dresser's symmetric arrangement of drawers translates into a point cloud pattern of evenly spaced rectangular indentations across a broad flat surface.
flower pot: A flower pot is a container typically made of terracotta or glazed ceramic, often seen with a plant or flowers growing out of the top.
Flower pots are usually cylindrical or tapered in shape, with a rim at the top, and they often appear in indoor or garden scenes on shelves, windowsills, or patios.
The surface of a flower pot may be painted, patterned, or left natural, with a drainage hole at the bottom sometimes visible when the pot is empty.
In images, a flower pot is recognized by its distinct truncated cone shape, textured earthy colors, and the presence of soil or a plant emerging from its opening.
The point cloud of a flower pot captures a hollow, roughly conical volume with a wide circular top opening and a narrower flat or curved base.

The 3D structure often exhibits a smooth, tapered wall from top to bottom, with the rim forming a slight overhang or lip, resulting in a symmetric radial distribution of points around the vertical axis.

The interior cavity may be partially represented as an absence of points, indicating a concave surface that curves downward from the open top, while the solid base appears as a dense planar cluster of points at the bottom.

Flower pots in point clouds show rotational symmetry, with points densely populating the outer shell and a clearly defined circular cross-section when sliced horizontally, often with a small hole at the center of the base for drainage.
Combining 2D visual and 3D structure, the familiar terracotta appearance in images translates to a point cloud that shows a truncated cone with a textured outer surface and a hollow interior.
A flower pot’s recognizable cylindrical shape with a flared rim in photos corresponds in 3D scans to a smooth rotational sweep solid with an open top and a flat bottom disc.
glass box: A transparent glass container with a hinged lid, often used to display jewelry or keepsakes, with visible reflections and polished edges.
A clear box made of six glass panels, allowing a full view of the colorful marbles stored inside from any angle.
A small glass display case with a mirrored bottom, used in retail to showcase high-value trinkets and collectibles.
A cube-shaped glass box with a removable crystal lid, sitting on a shelf and revealing a delicate porcelain figurine within.
The point cloud forms a hollow rectangular prism with six planar faces, where the top face may be missing or detached representing an open lid.
The geometry consists of thin planar sheets forming a cuboid enclosure with right angles at each corner, exhibiting orthogonal symmetry.
From a 3D scan, the object appears as a set of flat, orthogonal surfaces enclosing an empty interior volume, with point densities concentrated on edges.
The point cloud defines a box-like shape with sharp creases and planar facets, showing high spatial regularity and parallel opposite faces.
The visually clear, light-refracting cuboid has six planar faces arranged orthogonally, typical of a glass box captured in a sparse point cloud of surface patches.
This transparent enclosure with rectangular glass panes reveals its hollow interior and sharp geometric edges, which in a 3D scan manifest as a set of connected, flat boundary planes.
guitar: A stringed musical instrument featuring a large, curvy wooden body with a central sound hole, a long fretted neck, and six metal strings stretched over a dark fingerboard.

A classic acoustic guitar with a warm, polished spruce top, dark rosewood back and sides, a circular rosette around the soundhole, and a slender mahogany neck extending to a headstock with tuning pegs.
The instrument displays a familiar silhouette of two rounded bouts, a narrow waist, and a prominent pickguard near the soundhole, with shiny tuning machines and bridge pins clearly visible.
An electric guitar with a solid contoured body, dual humbucker pickups, a tremolo bridge, and a slim neck with dot inlays, often seen in vibrant solid colors or sunburst finishes.
The point cloud captures an elongated structure with a symmetric hourglass-like main body, separated into upper and lower bouts by a distinct waist indentation, and a long, straight cylindrical neck.

A 3D shape consisting of a flared headstock at one end, followed by a thin, rectangular-profiled neck that merges into a larger, smoothly curved hollow body with a flat top and a circular perforation.

The geometry reveals a bilaterally symmetric object where the central spine runs from the headstock through the neck to the end pin, with the body flaring outward into rounded shoulders and a wider lower bout.

From a top-down perspective, the point cloud shows a distinct outline of a figure-eight body, with the neck tapering evenly and a central sound hole creating a void surrounded by a dense ring of points.

This iconic instrument, which in 2D images is identified by its wooden finish and metallic strings, corresponds in 3D to a self-consistent surface with a precise waist curvature and a planar top that sharply transitions into rounded back edges.

Visually recognized by the glossy, sunburst-patterned body and six gleaming strings, the 3D point data reconstructs a thin-walled resonant chamber and a solid neck with a slight relief angle, confirming the object's acoustic design.
keyboard: A rectangular input device covered with a grid of small, labeled buttons, commonly black or silver with printed letters and symbols.
A computer keyboard features a standard QWERTY layout with rows of square keycaps, often illuminated from beneath and resting on a slim base.
The keyboard's surface shows a dense arrangement of distinct keycaps, each bearing alphanumeric characters, function labels, or media icons.
In typical desk setups, a keyboard appears flat with a slight curve, its keys organized into a main block, arrow cluster, and numeric pad.
The point cloud presents a flat, elongated slab with a uniform thickness and a regular array of small cuboid bumps projecting from the top face.
From a side view, the 3D scan reveals a thin, inclined wedge shape, higher at the rear and sloping downward toward the front edge.
The structure consists of a rectangular base plane supporting numerous equally spaced block-like protrusions that form staggered horizontal rows.
A distinct zoning is visible: a large central field of keys, a smaller right-side grid for the numeric keypad, and a continuous ridge separating them.
The dark rectangular frame with bright key symbols in photos aligns with a point cloud of a flat panel populated by a precise lattice of raised squares.
Images show a keyboard's orderly key matrix and slight typing angle, matching a 3D geometry of a tilted plane covered in evenly spaced small blocks.
lamp: A lamp typically has a recognizable base, stem, and shade that emit light when turned on.
In photographs, lamps often appear with a glowing bulb or shade, casting soft illumination on surrounding surfaces.
The visual identity of a lamp includes a smooth or textured shade, a slender or sturdy support, and a cord or switch.
From a 2D perspective, a lamp can be spotted by its bright, concentrated light source, often contrasting with darker backgrounds.
The point cloud of a lamp exhibits a vertical axis of symmetry with a wide base, a narrow stem, and an expanded top shade region.
A lamp's 3D structure consists of a compact cluster of points for the bulb, surrounded by a cone or hemisphere of points forming the shade.
Point distribution reveals a dense, circular pattern at the base, a thin cylindrical shaft, and a flared conical or dome-like canopy.
In a 3D scan, the lamp's geometry is characterized by rotational symmetry around a central axis, with distinct segmented parts.
The visual appearance of a lamp's glowing shade corresponds to a conical point cloud volume that diffuses light outward from a central bulb cluster.
The recognizable silhouette of a lamp in images matches the 3D point-cloud profile of a base supporting a flared upper structure, often with internal point density for the light source.
laptop: A portable computer with a flat screen that opens like a clamshell, revealing a full keyboard and a trackpad below the display.
The device often appears in silver, black, or gray with a glowing logo on the lid, and its screen shows a graphic interface when powered on.
From a top-down view, it looks like a thin rectangle with rounded corners and minimal surface features, while the side view reveals USB ports and cooling vents.
When open, the laptop's glossy screen displays vivid icons and windows, while its chiclet keyboard is backlit and the seamless trackpad sits centered below.
The geometry consists of two thin rectangular slabs connected by a cylindrical hinge along one edge, allowing them to form an acute angle when open.
The bottom slab is a slightly thicker block with a recessed area for a rectangular array of small spaced indentations representing keys, and a flat smooth zone for the trackpad.
When closed, the two parallel planar slabs fit tightly, creating a slim profile with sharp edges and uniform thickness, symmetrical about the hinge axis.
Seen from above, the point distribution is dense on the planar surfaces, with gaps at the hinge, and a distinct pattern of small rectangular key tops on the base slab.
The clamshell form factor, easily recognized in 2D as a folding screen-and-keyboard, corresponds to two planar 3D volumes connected by a hinge, with the screen part being a thin flat panel.

Visual cues like the centered keyboard and tracking pad are mapped onto the geometric layout of the base slab, where the keyboard indentations form a rectangular array offset toward the hinge, leaving space for the wrist rest.
mantel: A mantel is a decorative shelf above a fireplace, often made of wood, stone, or marble with a prominent horizontal ledge.
The mantel typically features a protruding shelf supported by vertical pillars or corbels on each side, framing the fireplace opening.
From a front view, a mantel often displays a symmetrical rectangular structure with ornate carvings or molding along the top edge.
In many homes, the mantel serves as a focal point above the fireplace, holding frames, candles, or decorative objects.
The 3D point cloud of a mantel shows a dominant horizontal planar surface extending outward, often with a slight overhang from the wall.
Below the shelf, the point cloud reveals two vertical or sloped support elements, creating a balanced, sometimes arched or stepped recess between them.
The overall structure is roughly a cuboid with a thickened top profile, exhibiting bilateral symmetry about the vertical midline.
In a side scan, the mantel projects forward from the wall plane, forming an L-shaped or stepped profile with a clear ledge and underside.
The mantel combines a visually prominent flat shelf visible in both images and point clouds, where the 3D geometry directly reflects the 2D appearance of a horizontal overhang.

The symmetrical arrangement of the mantel's solid supports beneath the shelf is evident in both its textured appearance and the spatial distribution of points, linking visual symmetry to geometric balance.
monitor: A monitor is an electronic display device with a large rectangular screen framed by a slim bezel, often mounted on an adjustable stand.
The front view shows a glossy or matte display panel, sometimes with a manufacturer logo and control buttons on the lower bezel.
In typical office setups, a monitor appears as a sleek, dark-framed rectangle with a vibrant illuminated screen and slender supporting neck.
From an angle, the monitor reveals a thin profile housing the display components, with ventilation grilles and connection ports on the back.
The point cloud captures a monitor as a large flat rectangular slab with sharp edges, supported by a narrow pillar-like stand atop a flat rectangular base.
Geometrically, the monitor consists of dominant planar surfaces: a primary large front plane, a parallel back plane, and thin side edges forming a thin box shape.
The stand typically has a vertical or slightly angled cylindrical column connecting the central or lower edge of the screen to a wider flat base for stability.
Front-facing LiDAR scans capture a nearly uniform dense point distribution across the screen plane, with sparse points on the bezel and stand due to occlusion or reflectivity.
The monitor's recognizable black rectangular bezel and bright screen correspond in 3D to a large planar face with a recessed display area and slightly protruding outer frame.
Visually, the monitor's thin profile and curved stand translate into a geometric composition of a slender slab and an L-shaped bracket, with points distributed along smooth continuous surfaces.
night stand: A small wooden table next to a bed, often featuring a flat top surface, a single drawer with a metal knob, and four tapered legs, commonly seen with a lamp or a book on top.
The night stand typically has a rectangular silhouette, with a warm wood grain texture and a smooth lacquered finish, providing a compact storage unit beside the bed.
From a frontal view, a night stand presents a drawer face above a lower shelf or open space, with the top surface often extending slightly beyond the supporting legs.
In an image, a night stand is recognized by its modest height, matching the top of a mattress, and its frequent role as a perch for a bedside lamp, alarm clock, or phone.
The 3D shape consists of a large flat horizontal rectangle as the top, four vertical rectangular prisms as legs at the corners, and a distinct protruding rectangular block for the drawer front.
In a point cloud, the night stand exhibits a clear planar top surface, vertical side panels, and a front-facing drawer region that creates a recessed bounding box offset from the main body.
The geometry is characterized by bilateral symmetry along the front-to-back axis, with the drawer centered between two side panels, and the legs forming a stable base.
From a point cloud perspective, the night stand shows a stack of horizontal and vertical planar patches: the tabletop, the drawer front, the side skirts, and the floor plane with leg points.

Visually the top surface appears as a smooth plane, which in 3D corresponds to a dominant horizontal planar region often supporting other objects; the drawer front manifests as a slightly recessed vertical rectangle with a protruding cylindrical knob.
The night stand's recognizable 2D appearance—a rectangular block with a handle and a flat top—directly maps to its 3D structure: a cuboid body with a frontal inset and a thin horizontal slab on top.
person: A person is a human figure with a head, torso, two arms, and two legs, often seen in upright standing or walking poses.
Common visual cues include facial features like eyes, nose, mouth, and hair, along with clothing that varies in color and texture.
A person can be recognized by the distinct silhouette of shoulders, arms, and legs against backgrounds in photographs or videos.
In images, people appear in many activities such as sitting, running, or gesturing, often with hands and feet visible.
The 3D shape of a person consists of a roughly cylindrical torso connected to elongated limbs and a spherical head region.
The point distribution is dense on the head and torso, with arms typically extending outward and legs supporting the body, creating a balanced upright structure.
From a geometric perspective, a person exhibits bilateral symmetry along the vertical axis, with limb joints creating articulated segments.
In a point cloud representation, the head is a compact cluster of points, the torso a broader central mass, and the limbs are thinner, stick-like point sets.
The visual appearance of a person maps to a 3D structure where the head, shoulders, and hips form key anchor points for a articulated skeleton model.
Looking at a 2D image of a person, the silhouette translates to a 3D point cloud with a distinct vertical axis, bilateral mirrors, and limb lengths that match human proportions.
piano: A grand piano with a glossy black finish and an open lid reflecting light.
An upright piano with a wooden cabinet, often placed against a wall in a living room.
The recognizable pattern of black and white keys on a piano keyboard, tightly aligned.
A piano from a side angle, showing its large body, music stand, and pedals underneath.
The point cloud reveals a large flat top surface with a slight curvature on one side, characteristic of a grand piano.
The object features a distinct rectangular front section from which a series of thin, parallel rectangular protrusions represent the keys.
Three supporting legs are positioned symmetrically, with the third leg placed behind the curved edge of the main body.
The overall shape is composed of a wide horizontal board and a vertical soundboard, creating an L-like profile in 3D space.
The glossy texture visible in images corresponds to the smooth, planar surfaces that dominate the point cloud of the piano's body.
The visual prominence of the keyboard in 2D aligns with the dense, narrow rectangular structures extending from one side in the 3D scan.
plant: A potted houseplant with large, glossy green leaves radiating from a central soil-filled container.
A dense cluster of overlapping green leaves, some showing veins and delicate textures under natural light.
A tall stem with multiple small, oval leaflets arranged alternately, typical of a common flowering plant.
A vibrant green plant with a bushy shape, featuring small buds and dewdrops on its finely serrated leaves.
A sparse volumetric structure composed of many thin, flat surfaces loosely connected to a central vertical axis.
A hierarchical branching pattern where slender cylindrical stems diverge into smaller twigs, overall forming a tree-like topology.
An irregular point cloud with high local density in leaf clusters and low density along thin stems, lacking large solid regions.
A radially symmetric arrangement of planar-like leaf points emerging from multiple angles around a narrow central stalk.
Visually, the plant’s layered foliage creates a complex silhouette, which translates geometrically to a 3D point cloud with many overlapping thin sheets.
The green color and organic leaf textures seen in images correspond to a structurally irregular, fractal-like branching in the 3D point cloud.
radio: A radio typically appears as a rectangular or box-shaped electronic device with a front control panel featuring knobs, a tuning dial, a speaker grille, and sometimes a digital display.
Common visual cues include a prominent mesh or perforated speaker area, rotary volume and tuning controls, a power indicator light, and an antenna for signal reception.
Radios can be recognized in images by their distinctive combination of an audio output grille, a set of control knobs or buttons, and a frequency display, often encased in plastic or wood.
The object identity is conveyed through its audio-centric design, with a large portion of the face dedicated to sound output and the presence of a telescopic antenna or tuner.
As a 3D object, a radio is often a compact, mostly enclosed box with a dominant front rectangular face, a rear face that may be flat or slightly curved, and a hollow interior cavity for speakers.
The shape typically includes a main cuboid body with a protruding cylindrical antenna and smaller geometric protrusions for knobs and dials, creating distinct surface variations.
From a point cloud view, the radio exhibits a symmetrical layout along its vertical midline, with control elements placed on the front face and smooth untextured sides.

The point distribution reveals a dense planar region for the front panel, sparse points around the knobs and grille perforations, and a large void inside if scanned internally, indicating a hollow shell.
The 2D appearance of buttons and a speaker grille maps to a 3D geometry of recessed circular slots and a perforated plane, giving the radio a tactile functional structure.
Visually, a radio's rectangular front with control knobs corresponds to a 3D volume characterized by a flat front face with cylindrical pushable elements and a gridded surface for sound.
range hood: A stainless steel range hood mounted on the kitchen wall above a stove, featuring a rectangular canopy and a bottom panel with two mesh grease filters.
The appliance has a sleek metallic front with a curved shape, a flat underside containing aluminum baffle filters, and an integrated light.
A typical under-cabinet range hood shown in a kitchen image, with push-button controls, a slim stainless steel body, and a small LED panel on the bottom.
In a household photograph, the range hood appears as a metal box-like structure attached under a wooden cabinet, with a distinct rectangular vent opening.
The point cloud shows a large horizontal rectangular plate at the bottom with symmetrical elongated slots for filters, and a vertical back plate for wall mounting.
The shape consists of a trapezoidal prism that widens from a narrow top to a broader base, forming an inverted funnel with a flat lower face.
The geometry reveals a hollow interior beneath the canopy with strict bilateral symmetry along the central vertical plane and evenly distributed points.
The point cloud captures a cylindrical duct opening at the top and a flared bottom that transitions from a wide rectangle to a smaller rectangle, with a concave inner surface.
The metallic, smooth canopy surface visible in photographs corresponds in 3D to a gently curved, elongated hood with a uniformly thin but rigid spatial structure.
In images, the range hood's rectangular, low-profile body with rounded corners is mirrored in the point cloud as a broad flat box with a distinctly recessed bottom grid.
sink: A kitchen sink featuring a metallic basin, a curved faucet, and a drain opening, often set into a countertop.
A white porcelain bathroom sink with a visible drain, faucet holes, and a soap dish, commonly seen in residential interiors.
A double-basin stainless steel sink with a goose-neck faucet and handles, surrounded by a tiled backsplash.
A rectangular bathroom sink with a smooth basin, a mirror above, and a marble countertop, reflecting ambient light.
The geometry consists of a concave rectangular container with a flat rim around the top and a small cylindrical void at the center of the basin floor.
The point cloud captures a symmetrical bowl shape with steep sloping sides, a distinct circular drain depression, and a horizontal flange.
The structure includes a deep central cavity with uniform wall thickness and a separate elongated vertical pipe form protruding from the rear.
The spatial layout features a large elliptical depression with a sharp boundary at the rim and a precisely located circular hole near one short edge.
The glossy white ceramic basin appears in the point cloud as a smoothly curved, low-curvature interior with a crisp rim outline and a dark circular drain.
The metallic sink's reflective surface translates to a densely sampled shallow bowl with a curving spout form, creating a distinctive silhouette in both images and 3D scans.
sofa: A sofa is a large upholstered seat typically placed in a living room, featuring a backrest, armrests, and multiple seat cushions for comfort.
The sofa is often covered in fabric or leather and can be recognized by its long seating surface, soft pillows, and a sturdy frame forming the back and sides.
From a side view, a sofa appears as a low rectangular profile with a slightly inclined backrest and thick cushioned armrests at the ends.
In images, sofas are usually seen with an arrangement of throw pillows, standing on short legs or a plinth, and dominating the center of a seating area.
In 3D, the sofa's point cloud reveals a dominant rectangular volume with a planar seat surface, a vertical backrest plane, and blocky armrest protrusions on both sides.
The point distribution shows a roughly symmetric L-shaped cross-section along the longitudinal axis, with dense points on the seat and sparse points under the sofa where legs create gaps.
The sofa's geometry consists of a large flat horizontal region for the seat, a tall vertical panel for the back, and two boxy lateral blocks for the armrests, often with rounded edges.

A point-cloud scan captures the sofa as a cluster of points forming three main orthogonal planes: the seat base parallel to the ground, the back perpendicular to it, and the armrests rising vertically at the ends.
Visually, a sofa's soft, plush appearance corresponds to its 3D geometry of a large cushion-filled rectangular frame, with the high back and low seat forming a characteristic L-shaped profile.
The upholstered surfaces of a sofa, which appear smooth and continuous in photographs, translate into a 3D point cloud as densely sampled planar regions outlining the seat, back, and armrests.
stairs: A staircase is often depicted as a series of rectangular steps with distinct treads and risers, sometimes accompanied by a continuous handrail and balusters on one or both sides.
In many images, stairs appear as an ascending sequence of horizontal and vertical planes, often made of wood, concrete, or carpet, with visible nosing on each step.
Stairs can be recognized by the repetitive pattern of shadows and highlights across the step edges, often viewed from an angle that reveals the depth of each tread.
Typical visual cues include a strong diagonal handrail line and the parallel alignment of multiple step edges converging at a vanishing point in perspective.
The point cloud of stairs forms a stepped surface composed of multiple parallel horizontal planes at increasing heights, separated by near-vertical riser faces.
A 3D scan of stairs reveals a repetitive geometric pattern: a set of evenly spaced aligned rectangles stacked along an inclined axis, creating a saw-tooth profile.
In point-cloud representation, stairs consist of a dense planar distribution of points on the treads and sparse vertical riser regions, often with a sloped ground surface beneath.
The structure is characterized by bilateral symmetry along the direction of ascent, with the entire step sequence bounded by two lateral planes that may include railing line features.
Visually, stairs display a sequence of alternately lit horizontal and vertical faces; geometrically, this translates to a 3D point set with step-like height jumps and planar patches.

The combination of image-based texture on treads and risers with the underlying geometric stair-step pattern allows recognition as alternating flat platforms connected by vertical rises, forming a continuous path upwards.
stool: A simple wooden stool featuring a circular seat and four straight legs, often seen in rustic settings.
A modern bar stool with a padded round seat and a metal footrest, commonly used at kitchen counters.
A low three-legged stool with a sculpted seat, typical of traditional African designs.
A folding stool with a canvas seat and crossed metal legs, visible in profile with compact structure.
The 3D point cloud shows a horizontal circular surface supported by four vertical cylindrical shafts, each ending in a flat base.
The shape is radially symmetric about a central vertical axis, with a flat top and evenly spaced legs around the perimeter.
From a side view, the stool appears as a thick horizontal slab atop narrow parallel columns, with a gap between legs.
Point density reveals a continuous planar seat, sparse leg volumes, and potential footrest rings connecting the legs midway.
The visual impression of a stool from a photo matches its 3D structure: a broad seat plane balanced on slender vertical elements.
Combining the 2D silhouette of a stool with its 3D geometry, the object is characterized by a dominant upper horizontal disk and underlying vertical supports.
table: The flat, rectangular top surface of a table is supported by four vertical legs, making it a central piece of furniture for meals or work.
Tables often feature a smooth, level tabletop with visible wood grain or material texture, providing a stable surface for placing objects.
A dining table is typically surrounded by chairs, with a symmetrical design and a polished or matte finish on its surface.
In an image, a table is distinguished by its horizontal plane elevated above the floor, often with straight or tapered legs and possibly a crossbar.
From a 3D viewpoint, a table consists of a dominant planar point cluster for the top and thin vertical clusters for legs at the corners or center.
The point cloud of a table exhibits symmetry along its long and short axes, with a clear separation between the top plane and the ground plane.
In 3D scans, the tabletop appears as a high-density planar region, while the legs form sparse vertical lines or columns connecting to the floor.
The geometry of a table in a point cloud includes a large, flat, rectangular or round surface and supporting structures that are typically perpendicular to it.
Visually, a table presents a smooth top with defined edges, which in point cloud data translates to a well-defined planar boundary with sharp drop-offs.
The recognizable flat top and legs of a table in photographs correspond to a planar point set and cylindrical or polygonal support clusters in 3D representations.
tent: A tent is a portable shelter made of fabric stretched over poles, often seen in campsites with a triangular or domed silhouette.
From a side view, a typical tent has a sloped roof and sometimes a vestibule area with a zippered door and mesh windows.
The fabric of a tent is usually brightly colored in oranges, blues, or greens, with visible seams and guy lines tied to stakes.
A tent's image often includes surrounding camping gear like a campfire, backpack, or sleeping bag, but its key feature is the peaked roof structure.
As a 3D point cloud, a tent consists of a main ridge line with two sloping planar surfaces meeting at an apex, forming a triangular prism shape.
The point cloud shows a symmetric, elongated structure with a high central peak, thin curved poles tracing arcs, and fabric sagging slightly between supports.
In a dome-style tent point cloud, there is a hemispherical or semi-cylindrical distribution of points with sparse gaps at the door opening and dense clustering along the ground perimeter.
Multiple intersecting planes define the tent's body, supported by long thin pole-like point clusters that form a frame, with fabric points draping from the poles to the ground.
The recognizable peaked silhouette in images corresponds to the 3D point cloud's sharp ridge line and two sloping planar faces that meet at a narrow crest.
Colorful fabric panels visible in photos translate to textured planar surfaces in the point cloud, while the frame poles become thin linear point clusters accentuating the tent's structure.
toilet: A toilet is a plumbing fixture consisting of a bowl-shaped basin, a hinged seat and lid, and a water tank, commonly found in bathroom interiors.
The toilet features a glossy white ceramic bowl with a smooth surface, an oval seat, and a rectangular tank mounted behind the bowl.
From a side view, a toilet displays a curved pedestal base supporting the bowl, with the tank extending vertically at the back of the fixture.
In typical images, a toilet appears as a bright white sanitary appliance with a large vertical tank and a horizontal seat, often set against a tiled bathroom wall.
The point cloud of a toilet reveals a symmetrical structure with a central vertical tank that merges into a wider, low bowl region, forming an L-shaped profile in side projection.
Geometrically, the toilet consists of a box-like tank perched atop a tapered pedestal that expands into a rounded, concave bowl with an open top rim.
The point distribution is dense along the smooth outer surfaces of the tank and bowl, with a gap at the top rim indicating the seat opening and an internal void inside the bowl.
In a 3D scan, the toilet exhibits mirror symmetry along its sagittal plane, with a prominent horizontal plane at the seat level and vertical planar components defining the tank faces.

The visual appearance of a glossy white toilet translates to a point cloud with smooth, planar faces matching the tank and bowl surfaces, while the dark seat opening creates a distinctive geometric void.
A toilet's recognizable image silhouette, with a tall tank and low elongated bowl, maps onto a 3D geometry characterized by a vertical rectangular volume atop a curving, concave basin structure.
tv stand: A low, wide piece of furniture with a rectangular top surface designed to hold a television, often accompanied by drawers or doors for storage.
This home furnishing typically features a central open compartment for media players, flanked by cabinet doors, and is finished in wood tones or black.
In images, a TV stand looks like a sturdy table-like structure with a smooth top, sometimes with metal handles and a cable management outlet at the back.
A media console with a horizontal emphasis, displaying a clean front façade, often with minimalist legs or a solid base, intended to anchor a living room setup.
The point cloud reveals a box-like volume with a dominant flat upper plane, vertical bounding walls, and internal concave regions representing open shelves or cabinets.
Geometrically, the object consists of a rectangular cuboid base, possibly with additional smaller cuboids removed to create shelf cavities, showing orthogonal symmetry.
A dense planar top surface is supported by side panels, with the overall shape approximating a right-rectangular prism with extrusions or cutouts for storage.
From scanned data, the TV stand presents as an assembly of parallel rectangular faces, with a long horizontal extent, and internal partitions visible as planar gaps.
Visually recognized as a smooth-fronted wooden console, the point cloud geometry confirms a hollow box structure with a thick tabletop and thin vertical dividers.
The image shows a symmetrical cabinet with a flat top for a TV, while the 3D scan reveals distinct planar surfaces and precise 90-degree angles characteristic of manufactured furniture.
vase: An ornate ceramic vase with a narrow neck, bulbous body, and decorative painted floral patterns.
A tall transparent glass vase filled with water and fresh-cut roses, standing on a wooden table.
A minimalist modern vase made of matte white porcelain, with a sleek cylindrical shape and subtle texture.
A rustic terracotta vase with a wide mouth, handmade with uneven ridges and earthy orange-brown tones.
The point cloud depicts a symmetrical vessel with a circular base, a smoothly tapering neck, and a flared rim that creates a concave lip.
The shape consists of an elongated central body with a distinct waist, a narrow opening at the top, and a flat bottom that provides stability.
This point cloud shows a bilaterally symmetrical object featuring a smooth, continuous surface that transitions from a wide lower belly to a constricted midsection and finally a small aperture.
The geometry includes a hollow interior cavity bounded by thin walls, with evenly distributed points capturing the object's curved silhouette and uniform thickness.
The vase combines a glossy, reflective surface typical of glazed ceramics with a 3D profile that curves outward from a narrow base into a wide belly before narrowing at the neck.
Visually, the vase appears as a solid of revolution with hand-painted blue motifs, which in the point cloud corresponds to a radially symmetric shape with dense sampling along the decorative ridges.
wardrobe: A tall rectangular piece of furniture with one or two hinged doors, often featuring handles or knobs, and sometimes a mirror on the door, used for storing clothes.
The wardrobe typically appears as a large wooden or laminate cabinet with a flat front, vertical lines, and visible hinges, providing clothing storage in bedrooms.
In a typical image, a wardrobe has a prominent door panel that may be solid or framed, with a base slightly off the floor and sometimes a top molding for decoration.
Recognizable by its human-height scale and symmetric double-door design, a wardrobe often has a dark or painted finish, and its interior may include hanging space and drawers.

The point cloud reveals a large, box-like volume with height significantly greater than width and depth, vertical planar surfaces forming the front, sides, and back, enclosing an empty cuboidal interior space.
Geometrically, a wardrobe is a rectangular prism with two smaller protruding rectangular prisms at the front representing doors, often with small cylindrical or handle-shaped clusters of points.
From above, the point distribution shows a symmetric rectangular footprint; the front face may have a slight gap between door segments, and points are denser on the outer surfaces due to scanning.

The 3D structure exhibits a vertical axis of symmetry, with a hollow interior that creates a concave point distribution when scanned from an open side, and possibly horizontal shelves represented as planar point clusters.
The visual appearance of a tall, rectangular cabinet with doors is matched by a 3D geometry of a large box with a clear internal cavity and planar door surfaces.
An image of a wardrobe showing a flat front with handles corresponds to a point cloud of a vertical rectangular solid with articulated door points and small handle-like protrusions.
xbox: The Xbox gaming console appears as a sleek, black rectangular device with a prominent circular power button on the front face.
A white Xbox logo is typically positioned on the top left or center of the device, serving as a recognizable brand identifier.
The front panel features a disc slot, USB ports, and a sync button, making it easy to identify as a home entertainment console.
Seen from images, the Xbox often sits horizontally, with a glossy or matte black finish and subtle green accents around the edges.
The point cloud captures a roughly rectangular box shape with dimensions approximately 30cm wide, 20cm deep, and 6cm tall.
A distinct circular indentation on the top surface corresponds to the power button, creating a concave region in the mesh.
The long vertical slot on the front face appears as a narrow gap in the point cloud where the disc drive is located.
Ventilation grilles on the back and top surfaces create a series of parallel linear indentations, disrupting the otherwise smooth faces.
The black rectangular shape seen in images corresponds to a flattened cuboid in 3D, with a subtle curve along the top edge that catches light.
The recognizable front panel with its circular button and slot translates into a planar face with a round hole and a long narrow slit in the point cloud data.
